title: scorzonera hispanica subsp. coronopifolia
intro: Scorzonera hispanica subsp. coronopifolia, also known as the Spanish scorzonera or the cornflower-like scorzonera, is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Scorzonera hispanica subsp. coronopifolia include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, improve digestion, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat a range of health issues.
